title: UPI Intent Payment Credential
description: Transaction-specific credential for UPI Intent payments. Generated by the business via their PSP during Complete Checkout. Contains an NPCI-compliant intent URI for mobile platforms and an optional QR code for desktop/web fallback. UPI Intent is stateless — no stored credentials or tokenization is required between transactions.

type.description: Discriminator value identifying this as a UPI Intent credential.

intent_uri.description: NPCI-compliant UPI Intent URI per UPI Linking Specification v1.7. Must start with 'upi://pay?' and include at minimum the 'pa' (payee VPA) parameter. Platforms MUST launch this URI using the operating system's implicit intent mechanism, allowing the OS to present available UPI apps (GPay, PhonePe, Paytm, etc.). On desktop, display qr_code_data instead.

transaction_reference.description: Unique transaction reference. Maps to the NPCI 'tr' parameter in the intent URI. Used by the business to correlate PSP webhook events to the UCP checkout session. Maximum 35 alphanumeric characters per NPCI specification.

qr_code_data.description: Base64-encoded QR code image (PNG or SVG+XML data URI) containing the intent_uri. Required for desktop and web platforms where the upi:// scheme cannot be launched natively. Platforms SHOULD display this when intent URI launching fails or is unavailable.

qr_code_string.description: Raw string payload of the QR code (typically identical to intent_uri). Platforms that generate their own QR code images may use this field instead of qr_code_data.

expires_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when this intent URI expires. UPI intents are typically valid for 5–15 minutes. Platforms SHOULD poll for an updated credential if the buyer has not completed payment before this time.
